title: China expands restrictions on export of dual-use products to Japan
summary: Tokyo protests the inclusion of 20 Japanese companies in Beijing's export control list, amid rising bilateral tensions.

title: Japan prepares for crucial match against Brazil in the Football World Cup
summary: The Japanese national team finalizes details, with key players recovered, while fans already gather at the match venue in Houston.

title: Kintetsu line train derails at Kyoto station affecting 85,000 passengers
summary: The Transport Safety Agency investigates the accident, which left the track suspended all day and caused major delays.

title: 29-year-old arrested for murder and robbery of a man buried at a company in Hiroshima
summary: Police found the body buried on the company premises and arrested an acquaintance of the victim, who denies the charges.

title: 岸本聡子 wins re-election as mayor of Suginami with a message of liberal hope
summary: The incumbent mayor defeated the liberal party in a campaign marked by a desire for more inclusive and creative policies.

title: Japanese Parliament begins debate on reducing number of seats with opposition absent
summary: The ruling party pushes political reform while the opposition criticizes the move as 'anti-democratic'.

title: 30-year prison sentence for arsonist who caused three deaths in Aichi
summary: The court determined that the defendant acted with eventual intent, in a case that shocked the town of Yatomi.

title: Volatile financial markets in Japan: weak yen and Nikkei closes with moderate gains
summary: The Japanese currency trades at 161 yen per dollar on expectations of a delay in rate hikes, while the Nikkei index rises 107 points.

title: Family of illegal detention victim sues state for judicial error in Daikawara case
summary: The plaintiffs argue that the detention of the former advisor was arbitrary and demand accountability from the judge who ordered it.

title: Imperial Household Law reform pits government against opposition
summary: The Constitutional Party accuses the executive of 'trickery' in the process, while the Innovation Party delegates to its top representative.

title: High school student in cardiorespiratory arrest after drowning at Niigata beach
summary: The young man was rescued from the sea but could not be revived, while two companions managed to escape unharmed.

title: World Cup match Japan vs Sweden records 35% audience share in Kanto region
summary: The round of 16 match reached a peak audience of 37.8% at the end of the first half, becoming the most-watched of the year.

title: Putin admits need to stabilize Russian fuel market amid gasoline shortage
summary: Long lines at gas stations in Moscow force the government to consider telework to ease the crisis.

title: United States and Iran agree to cease attacks and resume dialogue in Qatar
summary: Both sides seek to reduce tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz, following exchanges of attacks.

title: Actors Kazuya Kamenashi and Minami Tanaka announce their marriage
summary: The couple, known for their high aesthetic awareness, expressed their desire to value their way of being as individuals.

title: June local election results: Kakogawa mayor wins fourth term
summary: In nationwide elections, the outgoing mayor of Kakogawa describes his campaign as 'a fight against the invisible'.

title: Canada beats South Africa and becomes first team to qualify for World Cup round of 16
summary: A goal in extra time by Eustáquio secured the pass for the co-host team, which continues to make history in its first phase.

title: Serena Williams returns to tennis at age 44 in Wimbledon tournament
summary: The former world number one believes nerves are proof of passion in her anticipated return to competition.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela causes at least 1,450 deaths and collapse of 774 buildings
summary: The quake also triggered the closure of the country's largest refinery due to a massive blackout, worsening the crisis.

title: Japan plans first clinical trial of pig-to-human organ transplant by 2028
summary: The project aims to combat donor shortage and could mark a milestone in regenerative medicine.
